post_note: The post is Korean-only; there is no English post, so no /en/ link exists.
backfill_note: This run was executed on 2026-07-13. Its raw artifacts — the per-module compile logs and the .beam output — were discarded per the finite-disk policy, so run.sh + fixture/ (the 7 .ex demo modules and a minimal mix project) are committed to reconstruct the METHOD. Unlike the advisory-database runs in this repo, the compiler's type output is deterministic for a pinned Elixir: re-running on Elixir 1.20.x reproduces the same warnings verbatim, so results.json is a qualitative capability matrix + exit codes, not a drifting snapshot. No artifact hashes were captured at run time, so none are invented here.

environment.note: Type checking is a compile-time analysis and is OS-independent; the warnings reproduce on any platform running Elixir 1.20.x. The pinned Elixir version is the load-bearing variable here, not the OS.

subject.name: Elixir set-theoretic (gradual) type checker
subject.note: Pin 1.20.x in the post. Elixir's inference-based type checker has expanded release over release; the exact warnings captured here are the 1.20 behaviour and are less complete (or absent) on earlier versions.

method.modules: each fixture/src/<f>.ex was compiled standalone with `elixirc -o <tmpdir> src/<f>.ex`, and the compiler's type output was captured verbatim.
method.annotations: none — no @spec and no type annotations anywhere. Every warning is produced by inference alone, which is the point being demonstrated.
method.exit_codes: the build-failure semantics were checked three ways: `elixirc --warnings-as-errors` on an unused-variable control, and — via the minimal fixture/typedemo mix project — `mix compile` and `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`.

determinism.note: There is no external or live data source. The compiler's type output is deterministic for a pinned Elixir, so nothing here drifts the way advisory-DB counts do in this repo's other runs. Reproduction fidelity depends only on the Elixir version being 1.20.x.

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: discarded at run time (finite local disk)
raw_artifacts.note: The per-module compile logs and the .beam output were deleted after the run; run.sh regenerates them into a temp dir on demand. No hashes were captured at the time (this predates the run being packaged here), so none are invented — the committed fixture + harness are the reproduction, not fabricated digests.
